title: House Committees Propose Major Changes to 45Z Clean Fuel Credit and Rescind RFS Funding in Budget Package
content: On May 12, the House Ways and Means Committee and the House Committee on Energy and Commerce released draft proposals for a reconciliation budget package, dubbed the â€œbig, beautiful billâ€ by former President Trump. The House Ways and Means Committee proposed key amendments to the 45Z Clean Fuel Production Credit, including restricting eligibility to fuels made from feedstocks grown in the U.S., Mexico, or Canada, and changing how lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions are calculatedâ€”specifically excluding emissions from indirect land use change. It also proposes setting distinct emissions rates for fuels derived from various animal manures and extends the 45Z credit through 2031, while prohibiting access to foreign-influenced entities. Meanwhile, the Energy and Commerce Committeeâ€™s draft includes a repeal of Section 60108 of the Inflation Reduction Act, which had allocated $15 million for EPA initiatives supporting environmental testing, lifecycle GHG assessments, and grants for advanced biofuels. The repeal would rescind any unspent funds, arguing the section doesn't directly fund the RFS administration but only supports related data collection.
author: Analysts Account
